Today’s trail run felt good. It was long, but I enjoyed it. I didn’t use my AirPods at all, which helped me stay more present and really take in the sights and sounds of nature. The ambiance out there was calming. I found myself really appreciating the parts of the trail with natural slopes. I’m not a fan of the steps that when I came across those stair-like sections, I just took my time and moved carefully, especially on the downhill. At times I got a little too excited, my pace picked up into zone 3. That’s when I could really feel the effort kicking in. I noticed it and made a conscious effort to ease up - bringing it back to zone 2, and even down to zone 1 by slowing down or walking. For hydration, I took water or isotonic drinks whenever I passed a toilet or felt thirsty. It was enough to keep me steady. After the run, I drank Milo and chocolate flavoured GU gel to recover.

good pacing at the start of the race where i can maintain same pace towards the end. i still need to walk going up the hill but maintained a healthy pace. HR is maintained at zone 3-4 at the first half. side stitch at km 11 where i had to walk and jog at a slower pace till it went away.  compared to the last trail run, I enjoyed and run faster this time. effort is not very high due to maintained pacing.
Bumilis ako compare last time. Pero namax out ko ung hr ko. 80% zone 5 🤣 nakakapagod pero good run to! Ulit ulit
Di ako masyado tumingin sa relo i tried to just enjoy the trail. Ayun bandang 12km ata 140 bpm na base ko. From 13 din tinakbo ko nalang to simulate race 155-160 bpm na dyan.

Felt pretty good today. 👍🏻
Just before my run, while warming up, I noticed a light ticking sound in my right shin. It came with a bit of discomfort… not so painful, but not normal either. I googled it up and it seems like it might be a posterior shin splint. That made me more careful during the run. I tried to shift my weight and effort away from the sore spot, and I worked on fixing my posture. I also lowered my cadence a bit to reduce the impact. Before heading out, I rubbed some Rheuma Salve on my shin — the warm, cooling feeling really helped.
Now, I can still feel the shin splint a little. And while I was resting on the swing, my feet cramped up. Oh dear!. 😅

I feel heavy maybe because of the mala for dinner. I need to figure out how to jebs before run. My lower calves are still a bit sore. i changed my zone to to max 154 to test, i might change back if it does not feel easy. kelan ba ako bibilis at zone 2 ayo

Today’s run was a mix of effort, excitement, and some discomfort but overall 👍🏻 good.
Before heading out, I had a cup of Milo for energy (didn’t have dinner yet). I was really looking forward to doing a speed or tempo session at the running track. Fraulein and I did a 5-minute warm-up together to get things going.
I started the run easy — 1 km in Zone 1, then gradually picked it up. The second kilometer was mostly in Zone 2, and by the third kilometer, I was in Zone 4. That’s when things got intense. I started to feel a weird discomfort that spread from my mid-back to my stomach. On top of that, I was hungry and had the urge to poop. 😅
My heart rate peaked at around 170 bpm, and I could definitely feel it,.. my heart was pounding! I decided to dial things back a bit after that. I was still able to breathe, and my legs felt okay, but my body was heating up fast.
Despite the tummy drama, I listened to my body, adjusted the pace, and didn’t overdo it…

Tonight, I felt extra hot. 🔥
I had a heavy dinner around 7:30 PM at Mayflower Hawker Centre, along with an iced Kopi C. 😅 After that, I walked home, got my things ready for Saturday’s Mandai walk, and passed them to Fraulein before heading out for an easy run at around 10:10 PM.

During the run, I started off with my usual slow pace (Pace 1). After about 1 km, I gradually picked it up to Pace 2 and 3.

Around the 3rd km, I started to feel some stomach discomfort (like I needed to poop)  but I kept running. The feeling went away for a while but came back again near the 4 km mark and lasted a few minutes. I also felt a bit of pain in my lower right abdomen and some discomfort in my posterior right shin. Thankfully, both went away after a while.

I perspired a lot more than usual during this run. At one point, I had to walk fast to the water station just to get a proper drink.

And then… oh dear. 😅
My Strava app didn’t upload the run because my phone was locked! 🤦🏻‍♀️ It gave me a notification saying I can manually record the activity., it was a little frustrating!

My split sucks!! i think i need to add pace in my training to pace hr too. I tend to go faster in the first 2km. I think that contributes to my maintained elevated HR in km 3 onwards.  It’s really frustrating because even if i run slowly, my Hr will go over zone 2 after just 1 min, then i have to walk for 2-3 min to lower it down.  oh well!  No pain today but it was hot AF.

Before the run:
I drank one and a half glasses of water and ate a protein bar.

During the run:
I felt good overall. At 3 km, I took my first GU gel (Mandarin Orange flavour). I drank water at the fountain and had some of my isotonic drink at 6 km. Then I took my second GU gel (Tri-berry flavour) at 10 km.

My knees felt a bit sore at times, but the pain went away. I did some arm rolls to reset my form and help ease the tension. I also felt slight pain in my left ankle around 8 km, but it faded too. I did some wrist circles to stay relaxed.

I’m really happy I finished the run today! ❣️

I followed the training plan of 20 minutes in Zone 2, although I occasionally entered Zone 3. During the 5-minute Zone 3 segment, my heart rate briefly spiked into Zone 4, but I managed to bring it back down. I’ve noticed that I can sustain Zone 4 only up to a maximum heart rate of 168 bpm.

While running in Zone 4, I suddenly felt the urge to urinate and had to pause briefly to control it. 😅 I also experienced some back discomfort during this effort. I didn’t attempt Zone 5 (170–180 bpm), as I still don’t feel ready to push that hard yet. ✌🏻

After the run, I did a 15-minute post-run stretching session to help with recovery. I also drank a small bottle of coconut water to rehydrate.

I started my run almost an hour later. My heart rate stayed in Zone 1 during the first 10 minutes, then shifted back and forth between Zones 2 and 3 for the remainder of the run.

I felt heavy during the run (hehe), and around the 4th kilometer, I got very thirsty. I stopped for a quick drink at the water fountain, and it gave me a boost of energy.

No pain was felt in my shins, knees, or legs — which is great. I just didn’t maintain a steady or consistent breathing rhythm, possibly because I was getting distracted by the songs I was listening to.

Poor Sleep the night before. Di ako makatulog halong excitement at kaba.3am wakeup call. 4:15 nasa venue na kami. Sobrang humid, sa dami ng tao. Warmup nagawa ko naman pero pawis na agad. Sa starting pen 115 hr na agad ako. Flag off ayun na. 145 na agad hr ko at 630 pace. Sobra pressure ko sa sarili ko, ang sikip ng daan masyado, dikit dikit and again, grabe humidity, combined with the amout of people around you. 8km wala nilagpasan nako ng 2:05 pacer nasa 150-160 hr nako neto green zone. Gusto ko sana habulin, tanaw ko pa sila all the way to km15, pero from km8 palang nag 170 hr nako. Nag run walk nako, pero with pressure i only allow 10 sec of walk which did not calm me down enough. km15 wala na nilaban ko na tinangap ko na na hindi to sub 2, then nawala nadin yung 2:05 pacers. Pushes till 201 hr nung pa finishline na. Take away from this tune up race : respect the distance. I feel na yung fitness level ko is andun naman, kaya ko naman 6:30 pace sa 30km. pero this was ideal conditions. Race nerves, humidity, people crowding is another story that could spike core body temperature. We will try ko train for these conditions, like heat training and sodium + hydration. 8 weeks to go, expectations are low now.

PB 4:41 official time. wow super happy with this race. This is the firstime im writing a log for a marathon on a positive tone. Everything went well. I have 4 phases in my strategy, 1-13km is 7:28 pace - naglalaro ako neto sa warmup ko, getting my rythym at wag mag over shoot and hr at body temperature. At this point nahabol ko yung 5:15 pacers agad, pero nalaglag ako kasi need mag toilet cost me maybe 5 mins. Then phase 2, 6:50 pace 14-30 km, felt good dito, all in control padin, nglaro sa 6:30-6:40 ang pace ko dito. Hindi ko pa inaabutan yung 5hr pacer neto. By km 35 ayun na inabot ko na si 5hr pacer, binanatan ko na, change playlist na to ratrat mode. 5:30 pace laban all the way to 40k, 41-42k kinaya pa mag 5:00 pace. A lot of things went well, mahangin sya sa pen palang, di ko ramdam yung pressure masyado, maganda tlga yung tuneup race- it humbled me. Sobrang realistic ng expectation ko this race kaya sobra saya ko sa 4:41 finish. Hindi masyado umaraw till 28km so maganda tlga temperature kaya mo maghubad ng cap para madaling mgbuhos sa ulo ng tubig. Ang daming cheering station lalo dun sa mahihirap na parts. Ang ganda ng placement ng aide station ang haba pa, ang daming food, i even managed to skip stations. Really happy with this one!

PB HM 1:57:02. Humidity 88% daw nakita ko post from someone, hahaha, other than that yung route was insane. Daming liko, sobra congested pa. So pag sub 2 attempt pala umpisa palang dapat ma hold mo na yung pace mo, at ang hirap nya gawin kung masikip. The whole time i was zigzaging never getting the rhythm. Mabuti nalang my body was ready, first km nasa 158bpm na agad ako, pero ayun nga alam ko na kakayanin ko, control lang, while still maintaining pace dun sa garmin pace pro na sinet ko. After 5km wala 5:30 na talaga yung hinold ko. Laban na 10km pala, hindi na tlga lumuwag daan. By km 14, nasa 5min pace nako. km 16 4:40 all out na wala na tinginan sa relo, by this time alam ko na na sub-2 na sya, pero ang hirap padin. 1:57 whooo. 3 min lang from 2 hrs pero yung effort is grabe pala tlga. Respect the distance and pace. super happy and proud of this. Next pb attempt kailangan magandang race talaga sya kasi race condition tlga nagpahirap dito, karamihan ng kakilala ko underwhelming yung mga results nila, si rhaven 1:54 eh nasa 1:40 na to. Si toti 1:55 din ata eh alam ko nasa 1:40 nadin ko, si lloyd 1:44 eh 1:33 ang pb nong loko na yun. Overall, pinahirapan kami lahat ng race condition. Mabuti nalang talga ready katawan ko. Naulit yung mga fear ko dun sa mga prev race ko eh. Before the race gutom nako, so nag gel na agad ako, (nangyare to nung las last marathon ko) tas yung crowd na sobra sikip (eto nung last garmin hm). Worst condition pero na sub 2 ko padin. Be humble, race condition = 50% harder than good conditions.
